rulings.Case Title: MARINA PERLS VS. CITI PROPERTIES 1,LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 31, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANTS CITI PROPERTIES I LLC, TROPHY PROPERTIES, VII, LLC, CITI APARTMENTS, INC., THE LEMBI GROUP, FRANK LEMBI, and, WALTER LEMBI MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion denied. Defendants do not set forth the specific causes of action, claims for damages or issues of duty in the notice of motion and in their separate statement. (CRC 3.1350.) Plaintiff's objections to evidence sustained. Defendants fail to shift the burden of proof. No evidence presented that the requested accommodation is unreasonable or unduly burdensome on defendants. No evidence specified and no argument presented regarding the ten causes of action contained in plaintiff's first amended complaint. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BRANDON KAPLAND et al VS. SARA FRAUSTO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Disqualify Deft'S Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 31, 2009, line 5, PLAINTIFFS BRANDON KAPLAND, SUSAN CLARK, and, KENNETH KAPLAN Motion To Disqualify Defendants' Counsel. Motion denied. Hall's representation of plaintiffs ended in June 2008. After one consultation, that did not concern the 3443-20th (Mission) Street property that is the subject of this litigation, Clark failed to follow through with the permit history and plaintiffs initiated no further contact with Hall who closed his file June 17, 2008. Plaintiffs fail to show the nature of any confidential information Hall obtained during the one consultation meeting and how it relates to the current litigation. Plaintiffs fail to show Hall provided advice on a legal issue losely related to an issue raised in this case. The Mission Street property was not discussed during the meeting. The adverse impact on defendants if Hall and Associates could not represent them would be substantial. Gutierrez has previously successfully represented them in an unlawful detainer involving the same apartment. =(302/CWW)
